mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS transmits lower discount rates and easier financial conditions to equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is moving down rather than sideways.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above a restrictive threshold, equity valuations compress and risk appetite weakens, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rates above neutral are restrictive for equities
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases over a walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in UNRATE signals weakening labor income and higher recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month once unemployment begins to trend higher.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the prior 3-month UNRATE trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in RSAFS indicates weakening consumer and business demand, reducing near-term revenue expectations and leading S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly RSAFS drops are followed by nonnegative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of triggered instances, or if Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO alongside stable labor conditions implies improving real activity and margin support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations catch up.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint pattern of rising INDPRO with stable or improving labor data does not outperform unconditional returns over 60 trading days, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth weakens while HOUST also softens, the combination signals broad demand deceleration and housing-led cyclical stress, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative and HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined GDP-housing contraction trigger does not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if either series alone explains the full effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend with flat or rising UNRATE reflects payroll deterioration before recession recognition, and that labor-market deterioration should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines in 2 consecutive monthly prints while UNRATE is flat or higher
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are positive in a majority of payroll-deterioration triggers, or if the signal vanishes after excluding months with large market-wide shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS still low relative to inflation implies real rates are too accommodative and forces the market to price a policy repricing, which should hurt S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays below 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration periods with low FEDFUNDS do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns on average, or if policy repricing effects are absent in walk-forward testing.
